mechanism_narrative: Sustained declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth, compressing corporate margins and triggering negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when UNRATE sustains below 5.0% for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer credit expansion combined with rising industrial production signals robust aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative IC when RSAFS and INDPRO both rise, or forward returns do not correlate with the interaction term.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts predict positive equity returns only during mid-cycle expansions when inventory cycles are aligned with consumer demand, reinforcing corporate earnings momentum.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when HOUST rises during mid-cycle expansions, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects underlying economic momentum, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week as earnings revisions turn favorable.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 5-day forward returns over a 5-year sample, or directional accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 2.5% year-over-year, inflation expectations become unanchored, prompting fears of aggressive Fed tightening that compress equity multiples over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL crosses the 2.5% threshold, or forward returns show no statistical difference from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong payroll growth and stagnant industrial production signals a services-led economy with lower capital intensity, boosting profit margins and driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-INDPRO divergence fails to predict positive returns, or IC becomes negative during manufacturing booms.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production drives positive equity returns exclusively during periods of supply-side productivity expansion, as capacity utilization improves without triggering demand-pull inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises fail to produce positive forward returns during supply-side productivity booms, or the signal reverses when consumer credit expands simultaneously.
